title: Quantized time in quantum walks under weak rank-K measurements
abstract: Measurements can be used to monitor the evolution of quantum systems and may lead to a universally quantized time statistics. It is known that the mean return time is quantized for strong and indirect monitoring through the winding number of the return amplitude in a one-dimensional space. Here we discuss that under multi-channel strong or indirect monitoring, where the latter is achieved through ancilla coupling, the mean return time of a quantum walk in the projected subspace is also quantized. This reflects a universal time quantization for a higher dimensional evolution.
summary: This paper theoretically examines the quantization of time statistics when monitoring a quantum walk using multiple channels of weak or strong measurements. The authors demonstrate that this multi-channel monitoring preserves the universal quantization property, showing that the mean return time is proportional to the winding number divided by the coupling strength parameter. This extends fundamental results on recurrence times to higher-dimensional monitored systems.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether monitoring a quantum walk evolution using multi-channel strong or indirect measurements leads to a universally quantized time statistic for the mean return time.
main_result: It is shown that under such multi-channel monitoring, the mean return time of the quantum walk in the projected subspace remains quantized, suggesting universal time quantization even for higher dimensional dynamics.
method: The analysis involves calculating transition amplitudes using matrix elements derived from the unitary evolution operator and projecting onto a $K$-dimensional subspace defined by projectors. The key mathematical tool is relating the mean return time to the winding number of these amplitudes in the complex plane.
model_or_system: The model is a quantum walk whose evolution is monitored by repeated measurements, which can be modeled as direct projection or indirectly via an ancilla coupling. The system's dynamics are analyzed within a projected $K$-dimensional subspace.
key_observables: Mean return time ($ ar{t}$), mean number of measurements ($ ar{n}$), and the winding number ($w$).
important_parameters: The coupling strength parameter $\eta$ (or related variable $x$), and the dimension of the projected subspace, $K$.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes orthogonal states $|\psi_langle$ and relies on specific algebraic manipulations involving matrix inverses.
paper_structure: The paper builds upon known results for mean return time quantization in 1D walks ($K=1$) and extends this framework to the general case of multi-channel monitoring ($K>1$), deriving relationships between $ ar{n}$, $w$, and $\eta$.
why_interesting: This work connects fundamental concepts from quantum measurement theory (weak measurements, projection) with quantum transport phenomena (quantum walks), providing a theoretical framework for observing quantized time statistics in open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
